title: Introduce storage classes, producer registry and budgets
goal: Add a machine-readable Heim-PC storage policy and a read-only inventory collector that attributes temporary and regenerable storage to producers, records size and age, and evaluates warning and hard budget thresholds.

approval.note: Source changes and read-only verification are authorized. Runtime deletion or cleanup effects require a fresh exact-target plan and post-readback.

acceptance.assertion: Storage classes, producer paths, owner requirements, warning thresholds and hard thresholds are schema-validated.
A deterministic collector reports filesystem totals, registered producers, unowned large paths and daily growth input without deleting anything.
The current large paths are represented without claiming their cleanup is safe.
Fixture-based tests cover boundary thresholds, unknown paths and stable repeated output.

metadata.source: STORAGE-LIFECYCLE-V1 plan from live 2026-07-14 storage audit
does_not_establish: permission to bypass leases or dirty-state guards
validation: 21 focused storage inventory tests passed, including exact inclusive warning/hard boundaries and stable repeated output under a fixed observation envelope.
53 complete Heim-PC tests passed on the final verification head.
Syntax validation, repository-index consistency, git diff --check and canonical Metarepo contract validation passed.
GitHub smoke, guard and validate checks passed on both PR #42 and PR #43.
Post-merge readback proved PR #42 main tree byte-identical to the tested implementation tree; PR #43 added tests only.

completion.outcome: Heim-PC now has a machine-readable storage policy and a bounded read-only collector that attributes large temporary and regenerable paths, evaluates budgets, reports unowned candidates, publishes atomic retained snapshots and never grants cleanup authority.

verified_acceptance.policy-contract: Classes, producer paths, owners, exact inclusive budgets, filesystem thresholds and safety invariants are fail-closed validated; automatic cleanup must remain false.
verified_acceptance.inventory: The collector reports filesystem totals, six registered producers, unowned large worktree candidates, size/age fields and retained time-series input without deleting or authorizing deletion.
verified_acceptance.baseline: The live scan represented the current large paths, reported two hard limits and one warning, found one unowned candidate, and kept every cleanup authorization false.
verified_acceptance.tests: Fixture tests cover threshold boundaries, unknown paths, symlink and special-file handling, atomic file/directory fsync, retention and stable repeated output.

runtime_verification_stamp.does_not_invalidate: GitHub, CI, Git tree and live inventory evidence above

metadata.verification_note: T001 is satisfied by two merged GitHub PRs, green local and GitHub validation, byte-identical post-merge tree readback and a head-bound live inventory. The separate Bureau runtime identity mismatch blocks only the optional official verification-stamp command and is registered as BUREAU-TRUTH-MODEL-V2-T015.
